mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending earnings contraction, causing investors to price in higher recession risk and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: The joint behavior of real GDP growth and core inflation determines the real policy stance; when GDP growth accelerates while CPI remains stable, corporate profit margins expand without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression over a 5-year out-of-sample period, or forward returns are flat when both series move in the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects robust manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth decouples from equity returns during supply-chain normalization periods, resulting in a negative or zero correlation over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and consumer spending capacity, proxied by RSAFS, indicate strong household balance sheets that sustain consumption-driven earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when RSAFS declines, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of high inflation eroding real purchasing power.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls growth exceeds 200000 per month while the unemployment rate remains below 4.5%, wage pressures accelerate without triggering a recession, allowing firms to pass costs to consumers and supporting equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage growth
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when wage inflation spikes above 4%, causing the Fed to pivot to aggressive tightening and compressing equity multiples.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals a cooling in the broader construction and durable goods sectors, which leads to a slowdown in aggregate demand and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts decline but equity returns remain positive due to offsetting strength in services consumption, breaking the historical correlation over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The spread between the federal funds rate and core inflation determines the real cost of capital; when the real rate turns deeply negative, liquidity conditions ease and drive positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity returns decline when real rates turn negative due to stagflation fears dominating valuation models, or the spread shows no incremental predictive power over nominal rates alone.
